A.rationale: Mind Map A covers most of the paper but struggles with logical hierarchy, such as separating 'Task Inference' from 'Few-shot methods' despite their relationship in the text. It also extracts contextless trivia (e.g., '1987 年', '611% 中位数得分') instead of focusing on core theoretical concepts, and largely misses the comprehensive 'Supervision' section.

B.rationale: Mind Map B perfectly aligns with the tutorial's structure, accurately categorizing Few-shot methods (PPG, Black Box, Task Inference) and Many-shot methods. The hierarchy is highly logical, nodes are concise yet descriptive (explaining core mechanisms of algorithms like MAML, RL2, and PEARL), and it comprehensively covers all key dimensions including exploration, supervision, and open challenges.
